name: feature-right-click-context-20260404-0347
idea: Feature: Right-click context menu for selected gallery/timeline items with Create Shot and Generate Video actions. When items are selected (in gallery or timeline), right-clicking should show a context menu with: (1) 'Create Shot' — creates a new shot containing the selected items (images/videos). Should work for single or multiple selected items. (2) 'Generate Video' — creates a new shot with the selected items, then opens the video generation modal (the one that appears in shot view) pre-populated with that newly created shot. Both actions should work regardless of whether items were selected in the gallery, timeline, or across both. The context menu should appear on right-click on any selected item. Also need to ensure there are good paths/flows for creating shots from selected items via buttons (not just right-click) and for generating videos from those shots.

overrides.note: User answers: Q1: Create shot then open the same VideoGenerationModal used in the shot list view on Video Travel Tool page (triggered by video icon button). Just create the shot and open that modal. Q2: Context menu should NOT show for non-media clips (no text, effects, audio). Q3: Use existing auto-naming convention — the app already auto-names shots (e.g. 'Shot X') when adding from gallery lightbox. Use that same pattern. Q4: Items should be POSITIONED — ordered based on their order on the timeline, or if from gallery, the order they were selected in. Q5: Skip floating action bar — just do context menu for now, figure out what makes sense for buttons later.
